# OPC UA CLI — Full Documentation

> Command-line tool for OPC UA servers. Browse, read, write, watch, discover endpoints, manage certificates, and generate PHP code from NodeSet2.xml — all from the terminal. Built on php-opcua/opcua-client. No framework dependencies.

Package: php-opcua/opcua-cli
Repository: https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-cli
Packagist: https://packagist.org/packages/php-opcua/opcua-cli
License: MIT
PHP: >= 8.2
Dependencies: php-opcua/opcua-client, ext-openssl

---

## 1. Introduction

`php-opcua/opcua-cli` is a command-line tool for interacting with OPC UA servers. It wraps the [`php-opcua/opcua-client`](https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-client) library into composable single-shot commands that work with Unix pipes and JSON output.

### Installation

```bash
composer require php-opcua/opcua-cli
```

Global installation:
```bash
composer global require php-opcua/opcua-cli
```

Binary location: `vendor/bin/opcua-cli` (local) or `opcua-cli` (global).

### Quick Start

```bash
# Discover what's available
opcua-cli endpoints opc.tcp://localhost:4840

# Browse the address space
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840

# Read a value
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "i=2259"

# Write a value
opcua-cli write opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" 42 --type=Int32

# Watch a value in real time
opcua-cli watch opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001"
```

### Design Principles

- **Thin CLI layer**: business logic lives in php-opcua/opcua-client, this package handles argument parsing, command dispatch, and output formatting only
- **No framework**: no Symfony Console, no Laravel — zero runtime dependencies beyond opcua-client and ext-openssl
- **Command pattern**: each command implements CommandInterface and is registered in Application
- **Output strategy**: all commands write through OutputInterface — ConsoleOutput for humans, JsonOutput for machines
- **Unix composability**: single-shot commands, JSON output mode, pipe-friendly (`| jq`, `| grep`, `> file`)

### Architecture

```
bin/opcua-cli                         # Entry point script
src/
├── Application.php                   # Main app, command registration, argv routing
├── ArgvParser.php                    # CLI argument and option parsing (no deps)
├── CommandRunner.php                 # Client lifecycle, security/auth/trust config
├── CodeGenerator.php                 # NodeSet2.xml → PHP code generation
├── NodeSetParser.php                 # NodeSet2.xml XML parsing
├── NodeSetXmlBuilder.php             # Address space → NodeSet2.xml export
├── StreamLogger.php                  # PSR-3 logger writing to stream
├── Commands/
│   ├── CommandInterface.php          # Contract (getName, getDescription, getUsage, execute, requiresConnection)
│   ├── BrowseCommand.php             # Browse address space
│   ├── ReadCommand.php               # Read node values
│   ├── WriteCommand.php              # Write values to nodes
│   ├── EndpointsCommand.php          # Discover server endpoints
│   ├── WatchCommand.php              # Watch value changes in real time
│   ├── GenerateNodesetCommand.php    # Generate PHP from NodeSet2.xml
│   ├── DumpNodesetCommand.php        # Dump address space to NodeSet2.xml
│   ├── TrustCommand.php              # Trust a server certificate
│   ├── TrustListCommand.php          # List trusted certificates
│   └── TrustRemoveCommand.php        # Remove a trusted certificate
└── Output/
    ├── OutputInterface.php           # Output contract (writeLine, writeData, writeTree, writeError, writeTable)
    ├── ConsoleOutput.php             # Human-readable tree/text output with ANSI colors
    └── JsonOutput.php                # Machine-readable JSON output
```

---

## 2. Commands

### browse — Browse the address space

Displays the children of a node in tree or flat format. Accepts NodeId strings or human-readable paths.

```bash
# Browse Objects folder (default)
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840

# Browse by path
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 /Objects/MyPLC

# Browse by NodeId
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1000"

# Recursive browse with depth limit
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 /Objects --recursive --depth=3

# JSON output
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 /Objects --json
```

Console output:
```
├── Server (i=2253) [Object]
├── MyPLC (ns=2;i=1000) [Object]
│   ├── Temperature (ns=2;i=1001) [Variable]
│   └── Pressure (ns=2;i=1002) [Variable]
└── DeviceSet (ns=3;i=5001) [Object]
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--recursive` | Browse recursively (tree view) |
| `--depth=N` | Maximum depth for recursive browse (default: 3) |

### read — Read a node value

Reads a single attribute from a node. Default attribute is Value.

```bash
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "i=2259"
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" --attribute=DisplayName
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" --json
```

Console output:
```
NodeId:     ns=2;i=1001
Attribute:  Value
Value:      23.5
Type:       Double
Status:     Good (0x00000000)
Source:     2026-03-24T15:30:00+00:00
Server:     2026-03-24T15:30:00+00:00
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--attribute=NAME` | Attribute to read: Value (default), DisplayName, BrowseName, DataType, NodeClass, Description, AccessLevel, NodeId |

### write — Write a value to a node

Writes a value to a node. Supports explicit type or auto-detection.

```bash
# With explicit type
opcua-cli write opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" 42 --type=Int32

# Auto-detect type (read-before-write)
opcua-cli write opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" 42

# Boolean
opcua-cli write opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=2000" true --type=Boolean
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--type=TYPE` | Explicit OPC UA type: Boolean, SByte, Byte, Int16, UInt16, Int32, UInt32, Int64, UInt64, Float, Double, String |

Supported type casting:
- Boolean: "true"/"false", "1"/"0"
- Numeric types: parsed from string
- Without --type: auto-detects by reading the node's DataType first

### endpoints — Discover server endpoints

Lists all endpoints with their security policies and authentication methods.

```bash
opcua-cli endpoints opc.tcp://localhost:4840
```

Console output:
```
Endpoint: opc.tcp://localhost:4840
Security: None (mode: None)
Auth:     Anonymous, UserName

Endpoint: opc.tcp://localhost:4840
Security: Basic256Sha256 (mode: SignAndEncrypt)
Auth:     Anonymous, UserName, Certificate
```

### watch — Watch a value in real time

Monitors a node value continuously. Two modes: subscription (default) and polling.

```bash
# Subscription mode — server pushes changes
opcua-cli watch opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001"

# Polling mode — read every 250ms
opcua-cli watch opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" --interval=250
```

Output:
```
[15:30:00.123] 23.5
[15:30:00.625] 23.6
[15:30:01.127] 23.4
^C
```

Subscription mode creates an OPC UA subscription and monitored item. Polling mode calls read() in a loop. Press Ctrl+C to stop.

### generate:nodeset — Generate PHP classes from NodeSet2.xml

Parses a NodeSet2.xml file and generates typed PHP code. No server connection required.

```bash
opcua-cli generate:nodeset path/to/Opc.Ua.Di.NodeSet2.xml \
  --output=src/Generated/Di/ --namespace=App\\OpcUa\\Di
```

Generated files:
- **NodeId constants** — one class with all node IDs as string constants
- **PHP enums** — `BackedEnum` for each OPC UA enumeration type
- **DTOs** — readonly classes with typed properties for structured DataTypes
- **Codecs** — `ExtensionObjectCodec` implementations for binary encoding/decoding
- **Registrar** — batch-registers all codecs with the client, handles dependency resolution

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--output=PATH` | Output directory (default: `./generated/`) |
| `--namespace=NS` | PHP namespace (default: `Generated\\OpcUa`) |

Features:
- Handles ValueRank (scalar vs array fields)
- Handles IsOptional (nullable properties)
- Sanitizes and deduplicates constant names
- Resolves alias DataTypes from XML
- Parses RequiredModel dependencies for registrar chaining

### dump:nodeset — Export server address space to NodeSet2.xml

Connects to a server, browses the address space, and exports it as a NodeSet2.xml file. The output can be fed directly to `generate:nodeset`.

```bash
opcua-cli dump:nodeset opc.tcp://192.168.1.100:4840 --output=MyPLC.NodeSet2.xml
opcua-cli dump:nodeset opc.tcp://192.168.1.100:4840 --output=MyPLC.NodeSet2.xml --namespace=2
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--output=FILE` | Output XML file path (required) |
| `--namespace=N` | Export only this namespace index (default: all non-zero) |

Exports: UAObject, UAVariable, UAMethod, UAObjectType, UAVariableType, UAReferenceType, UADataType nodes with references, DataType definitions, and namespace URIs.

### trust — Trust a server certificate

Connects to the server, retrieves its certificate, and stores it in the trust store.

```bash
opcua-cli trust opc.tcp://server:4840 --trust-store=~/.opcua
```

### trust:list — List trusted certificates

Shows all certificates in the trust store with fingerprints and details.

```bash
opcua-cli trust:list --trust-store=~/.opcua
```

### trust:remove — Remove a trusted certificate

Removes a certificate from the trust store by its fingerprint.

```bash
opcua-cli trust:remove ab:cd:12:34:... --trust-store=~/.opcua
```

Trust options (available on all commands):

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--trust-store=PATH` | Custom trust store path |
| `--trust-policy=POLICY` | Validation policy: fingerprint, fingerprint+expiry, full |
| `--no-trust-policy` | Disable trust validation for this command |

---

## 3. Security Options

All commands that connect to a server support full OPC UA security configuration.

```bash
# Username/password authentication
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://server:4840 "i=2259" -u admin -p secret

# Full security with certificates
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://server:4840 "i=2259" \
  --security-policy=Basic256Sha256 \
  --security-mode=SignAndEncrypt \
  --cert=/path/to/client.pem \
  --key=/path/to/client.key \
  --ca=/path/to/ca.pem \
  -u operator -p secret
```

| Option | Short | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--security-policy=POLICY` | `-s` | None, Basic128Rsa15, Basic256, Basic256Sha256, Aes128Sha256RsaOaep, Aes256Sha256RsaPss |
| `--security-mode=MODE` | `-m` | None, Sign, SignAndEncrypt (also accepts numeric: 1, 2, 3) |
| `--cert=PATH` | | Client certificate path (PEM) |
| `--key=PATH` | | Client private key path (PEM) |
| `--ca=PATH` | | CA certificate path (PEM) |
| `--username=USER` | `-u` | Username for authentication |
| `--password=PASS` | `-p` | Password for authentication |
| `--timeout=SECONDS` | `-t` | Connection timeout in seconds (default: 5) |

Security is configured via CommandRunner, which maps CLI options to ClientBuilder methods (setSecurityPolicy, setSecurityMode, setClientCertificate, setUserCredentials).

---

## 4. Output Options

### JSON

Add `--json` (or `-j`) to any command for machine-readable JSON output. Designed for piping to jq, scripting, or integration with other tools.

```bash
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 --json | jq '.[].name'
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "i=2259" --json | jq '.Value'
opcua-cli endpoints opc.tcp://localhost:4840 --json | jq '.[].securityPolicy'
```

### Console Output

Default mode. Human-readable output with tree formatting for browse, key-value pairs for read, and tables for endpoints. Supports ANSI colors when writing to a terminal (respects NO_COLOR env var and TERM detection).

### Debug Logging

Three debug logging modes, all using StreamLogger (PSR-3):

```bash
# Log to stdout (incompatible with --json)
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "i=2259" --debug

# Log to stderr (compatible with --json — data goes to stdout, logs to stderr)
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "i=2259" --debug-stderr --json

# Log to file
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "i=2259" --debug-file=/tmp/opcua.log --json
```

---

## 5. Global Options

| Option | Short | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--json` | `-j` | Output in JSON format |
| `--debug` | `-d` | Debug logging on stdout |
| `--debug-stderr` | | Debug logging on stderr |
| `--debug-file=PATH` | | Debug logging to file |
| `--help` | `-h` | Show help |
| `--version` | `-v` | Show version |

---

## 6. Main Classes

### PhpOpcua\Cli\Application
Main entry point. Registers all 10 commands, parses argv via ArgvParser, selects output adapter (ConsoleOutput or JsonOutput), delegates to CommandRunner.

### PhpOpcua\Cli\ArgvParser
Stateless argument parser. Extracts command name, positional arguments, and options (long --opt=val, short -o val, boolean flags). Handles all CLI option aliases (-j → json, -u → username, etc.).

### PhpOpcua\Cli\CommandRunner
Manages the OPC UA client lifecycle. Creates ClientBuilder with security, authentication, trust store, and logging configuration from parsed CLI options. Commands that require a connection get a connected Client; offline commands (generate:nodeset) get a ClientBuilder.

### PhpOpcua\Cli\Commands\CommandInterface
Contract for all commands:
- getName(): string — command name (e.g., "browse", "generate:nodeset")
- getDescription(): string — one-line description
- getUsage(): string — usage string with arguments and options
- execute(OpcUaClientInterface|ClientBuilder $client, array $arguments, array $options, OutputInterface $output): int — returns exit code
- requiresConnection(): bool — true for commands that need a connected client

### PhpOpcua\Cli\Output\OutputInterface
Output contract:
- writeLine(string $text): void
- write(string $text): void
- writeData(array $data): void — key-value pairs
- writeTree(array $nodes): void — tree structure
- writeTable(array $headers, array $rows): void
- writeError(string $message): void — to stderr

### PhpOpcua\Cli\Output\ConsoleOutput
Human-readable output. Tree rendering with box-drawing characters (├── └──). Key-value pair formatting. ANSI color support with automatic detection (posix_isatty, TERM, NO_COLOR). Color can be forced on/off.

### PhpOpcua\Cli\Output\JsonOutput
Machine-readable output. All data methods output JSON to stdout. Errors output JSON to stderr. Used when --json flag is present.

### PhpOpcua\Cli\CodeGenerator
Generates PHP source code from parsed NodeSet2 data: NodeId constants class, BackedEnum classes, readonly DTO classes, ExtensionObjectCodec classes, and GeneratedTypeRegistrar class. Handles type mapping, array fields (ValueRank), optional fields, enum casting, constant name sanitization and deduplication.

### PhpOpcua\Cli\NodeSetParser
Parses NodeSet2.xml files into structured data: namespace URIs, node definitions (UAObject, UAVariable, UAMethod, UAObjectType, UAVariableType, UAReferenceType, UADataType), DataType definitions with fields, enumeration definitions, encoding NodeIds, alias resolution, and RequiredModel dependencies.

### PhpOpcua\Cli\NodeSetXmlBuilder
Builds NodeSet2.xml documents from structured data. Used by dump:nodeset to serialize a server's address space to XML. Generates valid XML with proper namespaces, node types, references, and DataType definitions.

### PhpOpcua\Cli\StreamLogger
PSR-3 LoggerInterface implementation that writes formatted log messages to a PHP stream (stdout, stderr, or file). Includes timestamps and log levels. Used for --debug, --debug-stderr, and --debug-file options.

---

## 7. Code Generation Pipeline

The `generate:nodeset` command follows this pipeline:

1. **Parse**: NodeSetParser reads the XML file, extracts nodes, DataType definitions, enumerations, encoding NodeIds, aliases, and RequiredModel dependencies
2. **Generate**: CodeGenerator produces PHP files:
   - NodeId constants: one class with all node IDs as `public const string` constants
   - Enums: PHP 8.1+ BackedEnum for each OPC UA EnumeratedType
   - DTOs: readonly classes with typed properties (handles arrays via ValueRank, optionals via IsOptional)
   - Codecs: ExtensionObjectCodec implementations with read/write methods for binary encoding
   - Registrar: GeneratedTypeRegistrar that batch-registers all codecs, with dependency resolution for required models
3. **Write**: files are written to the --output directory under the --namespace

The `dump:nodeset` command reverses this:

1. **Connect**: establishes OPC UA connection to the server
2. **Browse**: recursively browses the address space (filtered by --namespace if specified)
3. **Read**: reads node attributes (DataType, ValueRank, IsAbstract, etc.) and DataType definitions
4. **Build**: NodeSetXmlBuilder assembles the XML document
5. **Write**: outputs valid NodeSet2.xml to --output file

The dump output can be fed directly into generate:nodeset for end-to-end code generation from a live server.

---

## Related Packages

- php-opcua/opcua-client: pure PHP OPC UA client library — the foundation this CLI is built on
- php-opcua/opcua-client-nodeset: pre-generated PHP types from 51 OPC UA companion specifications (DI, Robotics, Machinery, etc.)
- php-opcua/laravel-opcua: Laravel integration (service provider, facade, config)
- php-opcua/opcua-session-manager: session persistence across PHP requests

## Versioning

This package follows the same version numbering as php-opcua/opcua-client. Each CLI release is aligned with the corresponding client library release.

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-cli
- Documentation: https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-cli/tree/master/doc
- Issues: https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-cli/issues
- Packagist: https://packagist.org/packages/php-opcua/opcua-cli
- Changelog: https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-cli/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md
- Client library: https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-client
